test_cases.title: Graph integrity after node and edge add/delete cycle
preconditions: An empty clock tree graph exists
test_cases.given: An empty DAG with 0 nodes and 0 edges
test_cases.when: A PLL node (id='pll-1') and a Divider node (id='div-1') are added, an edge PLL1:out→Divider1:in is added, then the edge is deleted, and finally Divider1 is deleted
test_cases.then: After all operations the graph contains exactly 1 node (pll-1) and 0 edges; no orphan edges reference the deleted Divider1 node

test_cases.title: Node creation returns 201 and unique ID for all 6 component types
preconditions: A project with id='proj-1' exists and has an empty graph
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 exists with 0 nodes
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/proj-1/nodes is called once for each type: PLL (output_freq=100), Divider (ratio=2), Mux (select_index=0), ClockGate, IPBlock, ClockDomain
test_cases.then: Each request returns HTTP 201 with a body containing a unique node id; the project now has exactly 6 nodes with the correct type field for each

test_cases.title: Node creation rejected for unknown component type
preconditions: A project with id='proj-1' exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 exists
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with a JSON error body containing a message that identifies 'unknown component type' or equivalent validation failure description

test_cases.title: Node creation rejected when required properties are missing for each component type
preconditions: A project with id='proj-1' exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 exists
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/proj-1/nodes is called three times: (1) {"type": "PLL", "properties": {}} with output_freq missing, (2) {"type": "Divider", "properties": {}} with ratio missing, (3) {"type": "Mux", "properties": {}} with select_index missing
test_cases.then: Each of the three requests returns HTTP 400 with a JSON error body that identifies the specific missing required property: 'output_freq' for PLL, 'ratio' for Divider, 'select_index' for Mux

test_cases.title: Valid connection between PLL output and Divider input returns 201
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL node with id='pll-1' exists
Divider node with id='div-1' exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has PLL node pll-1 and Divider node div-1, and no connections exist
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 201 with a JSON body containing a unique connection id, source='pll-1:out', and target='div-1:in'

test_cases.title: Connection rejected when IP Block is used as source (no output port)
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
IPBlock node with id='ip-1' exists
Divider node with id='div-1' exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has IPBlock node ip-1 and Divider node div-1
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with an error message indicating IP Block does not have an output port

test_cases.title: Connection rejected when PLL is used as target (no input port)
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
Divider node with id='div-1' exists
PLL node with id='pll-1' exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has Divider node div-1 and PLL node pll-1
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with an error message indicating PLL does not have an input port

test_cases.title: Connection rejected when self-loop would be created on a single node
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
Divider node div-1 exists with no connections
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has a standalone Divider node div-1 with no existing connections
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/proj-1/connections is called with body {"source": "div-1:out", "target": "div-1:in"} (self-loop attempt on div-1)
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with an error message indicating a cycle (self-loop) would be created

test_cases.title: Connection rejected when target input port is already occupied
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL pll-1, PLL pll-2, Divider div-1 exist
Connection pll-1:out→div-1:in exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has Divider div-1 with its 'in' port already connected from pll-1:out
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/proj-1/connections is called with body {"source": "pll-2:out", "target": "div-1:in"} (same target input port)
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with an error message indicating the target input port 'div-1:in' is already occupied

test_cases.title: Connection rejected when source or target node does not exist
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists with no nodes
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has no nodes
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with an error message indicating one or both nodes do not exist

test_cases.title: Cycle detection rejects self-loop connection
preconditions: An in-memory graph with Divider node div-1 exists
test_cases.given: A graph containing a single Divider node div-1 with no edges
test_cases.when: The cycle detection algorithm checks whether adding edge div-1→div-1 would create a cycle
test_cases.then: The algorithm returns true (cycle detected) and the detected cycle path includes 'div-1'

test_cases.title: Cycle detection identifies 2-node cycle
preconditions: An in-memory graph with Divider div-1 and ClockGate cg-1 connected div-1→cg-1 exists
test_cases.given: A graph containing Divider div-1 and ClockGate cg-1 with edge div-1→cg-1
test_cases.when: The cycle detection algorithm checks whether adding edge cg-1→div-1 would create a cycle
test_cases.then: The algorithm returns true (cycle detected) and the detected cycle path contains both 'div-1' and 'cg-1'

test_cases.title: Cycle detection identifies cycle in a 3-node chain
preconditions: An in-memory graph with nodes A, B, C and edges A→B, B→C exists
test_cases.given: A graph with three Divider nodes A, B, C and edges A→B and B→C already present
test_cases.when: The cycle detection algorithm checks whether adding edge C→A would create a cycle
test_cases.then: The algorithm returns true (cycle detected) and the detected cycle path includes all three nodes A, B, C

test_cases.title: Second connection to Mux in_0 port is rejected (single-connection constraint per input port)
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL pll-1, PLL pll-2, Mux mux-1 exist
Connection pll-1:out→mux-1:in_0 exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has Mux mux-1 whose 'in_0' port is already connected from pll-1:out; pll-2 has no connections
test_cases.then: The response is HTTP 400 with an error message indicating the input port 'mux-1:in_0' already has a connection and cannot accept another

test_cases.title: Frequency propagation: PLL→Divider→IPBlock chain calculates correctly
preconditions: An in-memory graph exists with PLL pll-1 (output_freq=400), Divider div-1 (ratio=4), IPBlock ip-1 connected as pll-1→div-1→ip-1
test_cases.given: Graph has PLL pll-1 with output_freq=400MHz, Divider div-1 with ratio=4, and IPBlock ip-1 connected in chain pll-1:out→div-1:in, div-1:out→ip-1:in
test_cases.then: div-1 computed frequency is 100.000MHz (400/4) and ip-1 operating frequency is 100.000MHz

test_cases.title: Downstream frequency recalculates when Divider ratio changes
preconditions: Graph with PLL pll-1 (output_freq=400), Divider div-1 (ratio=4), IPBlock ip-1 connected pll-1→div-1→ip-1 exists; initial propagation already run so ip-1=100MHz
test_cases.given: Graph with PLL pll-1 at 400MHz, Divider div-1 ratio=4, IPBlock ip-1 showing 100MHz after initial propagation
test_cases.when: Divider div-1 ratio is changed from 4 to 8 and frequency propagation is triggered
test_cases.then: div-1 computed frequency is 50.000MHz (400/8) and ip-1 operating frequency is 50.000MHz

test_cases.title: Mux output is null when select_index is out of range of connected inputs
preconditions: Graph with Mux mux-1 (select_index=5) connected to 2 PLL inputs on in_0 and in_1 exists
test_cases.given: Graph has Mux mux-1 with select_index=5, connected inputs only on in_0 (from pll-1 at 100MHz) and in_1 (from pll-2 at 200MHz), so only indices 0 and 1 are valid
test_cases.then: mux-1 computed output frequency is null because select_index=5 is outside the range [0, 1] of connected inputs

test_cases.title: Unconnected Divider input results in null output frequency
preconditions: Graph with standalone Divider div-1 (ratio=4) having no incoming connections exists
test_cases.given: Graph with Divider div-1 (ratio=4) whose input port 'in' has no connection
test_cases.then: div-1 computed frequency is null

test_cases.title: Null frequency propagates from unconnected Divider to downstream IPBlock
preconditions: Graph with Divider div-1 (no upstream connection) and IPBlock ip-1 connected div-1:out→ip-1:in exists
test_cases.given: Graph with Divider div-1 (no upstream PLL connected) and IPBlock ip-1 downstream of div-1
test_cases.then: div-1 computed frequency is null and ip-1 operating frequency is also null

test_cases.title: Node deletion removes the node, cleans up edges, and sets downstream frequency to null
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL pll-1 (400MHz), Divider div-1 (ratio=4), IPBlock ip-1 connected pll-1→div-1→ip-1 exist; ip-1 frequency=100MHz
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has PLL pll-1 at 400MHz → Divider div-1 (ratio=4) → IPBlock ip-1 at 100MHz
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 204; the graph no longer contains div-1; no edges reference div-1; ip-1 operating frequency is null

test_cases.title: CDC analysis returns crossings when two Clock Domains are connected
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
ClockDomain cd-1 connected to IPBlock ip-1 and IPBlock ip-2 exist
ClockDomain cd-2 connected to IPBlock ip-3 exists
An edge from ip-1 to ip-3 creates a cross-domain connection
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has two Clock Domains cd-1 and cd-2; cd-1 feeds ip-1, ip-2; cd-2 feeds ip-3; there is an edge from ip-1 to ip-3 crossing domain boundaries
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with a non-empty list containing at least one CDC crossing entry with source_domain='cd-1', target_domain='cd-2', and the involved node IDs

test_cases.title: CDC analysis returns empty list when no cross-domain connections exist
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists with a single Clock Domain cd-1 feeding all nodes; no inter-domain edges exist
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has one Clock Domain cd-1 with PLL pll-1 and IPBlock ip-1 all within cd-1; no other Clock Domain exists
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with an empty list []

test_cases.title: Gating analysis returns correct gated/ungated counts and power reduction percentage
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
ClockGate cg-1 exists upstream of IPBlock ip-1, ip-2, ip-3
IPBlock ip-4 is not downstream of any ClockGate
Total of 4 IPBlock nodes exist
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has 4 IPBlock nodes (ip-1, ip-2, ip-3, ip-4); ip-1, ip-2, ip-3 are each reachable from ClockGate cg-1; ip-4 has no ClockGate ancestor
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with body {"gated_count": 3, "ungated_count": 1, "total_count": 4, "power_reduction_pct": 75.0}

test_cases.title: Project creation returns 201 with project ID and metadata
preconditions: The API server is running with an empty database
test_cases.given: No projects exist in the database
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 201 with a JSON body containing a unique id, name='TestProject', created_at timestamp, and updated_at timestamp; the project's graph is empty (0 nodes, 0 edges)

test_cases.title: Project creation rejected when name is missing
preconditions: The API server is running
test_cases.given: No specific preconditions beyond a running server
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects is called with body {} (name field absent)
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 400 with a JSON error body indicating 'name' is required

test_cases.title: Project save persists graph state and updates updated_at timestamp
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists with PLL pll-1 and Divider div-1 connected pll-1:out→div-1:in
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has 2 nodes and 1 edge; initial updated_at is T0
test_cases.when: PUT /api/projects/proj-1 is called to save the current state
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with updated project metadata where updated_at > T0; a subsequent database query for proj-1 returns exactly 2 nodes and 1 edge matching the saved graph

test_cases.title: Project load returns complete graph with all nodes and edges (round-trip)
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL pll-1 (output_freq=200, x=10, y=20) and Divider div-1 (ratio=8, x=100, y=20) connected pll-1:out→div-1:in have been saved via PUT
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has been saved with PLL pll-1 (output_freq=200MHz, position x=10 y=20) and Divider div-1 (ratio=8, position x=100 y=20) connected by one edge
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with a body containing exactly 2 nodes (pll-1 with output_freq=200 and x=10 y=20; div-1 with ratio=8 and x=100 y=20) and exactly 1 edge (pll-1:out→div-1:in), identical to the saved state

test_cases.title: Project list returns all saved projects with metadata and node count
preconditions: Projects proj-A (3 nodes) and proj-B (5 nodes) have been created and saved
test_cases.given: Two projects exist: proj-A with 3 nodes and proj-B with 5 nodes
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with a JSON array of exactly 2 items; each item contains id, name, created_at, updated_at, and node_count fields; proj-A has node_count=3 and proj-B has node_count=5

test_cases.title: Valid node property update recalculates downstream frequency
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL pll-1 (400MHz), Divider div-1 (ratio=8), IPBlock ip-1 connected pll-1→div-1→ip-1; ip-1 frequency=50MHz
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has PLL pll-1 at 400MHz → Divider div-1 (ratio=8) → IPBlock ip-1 at 50MHz
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with updated div-1 data showing ratio=16; ip-1 operating frequency is now 25.000MHz (400/16)

test_cases.title: Node property update rejected when ratio value is not in allowed set
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
Divider div-1 with ratio=4 exists
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has Divider div-1 with ratio=4
test_cases.when: PATCH /api/projects/proj-1/nodes/div-1 is called with body {"properties": {"ratio": 3}} (3 is not in {2,4,8,16,32,64,128})
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 400 with a JSON error body indicating ratio=3 is not a valid Divider ratio value

test_cases.title: Connection deletion returns 204 and sets downstream frequency to null
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists
PLL pll-1 (400MHz), Divider div-1 (ratio=4), IPBlock ip-1 connected pll-1:out→div-1:in (conn-id='conn-1') and div-1:out→ip-1:in; ip-1 frequency=100MHz
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has PLL pll-1 at 400MHz → Divider div-1 (ratio=4) → IPBlock ip-1 at 100MHz; the connection pll-1:out→div-1:in has id='conn-1'
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 204; the connection conn-1 no longer exists in the graph; div-1 computed frequency is null; ip-1 operating frequency is null

test_cases.title: Connection deletion for non-existent ID returns 404
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists with no connections
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 exists but has no connections
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 404 with a JSON error body indicating the connection ID was not found

test_cases.title: Node creation rejected when project already has 200 nodes (limit enforcement)
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists with exactly 200 IPBlock nodes already created
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 has exactly 200 nodes (at the maximum limit)
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 400 with a JSON error body indicating the maximum node limit of 200 has been reached and no new node is created

test_cases.title: Project deletion returns 200 and subsequent GET returns 404
preconditions: Project proj-1 exists with 3 nodes and 2 edges
test_cases.given: Project proj-1 exists with 3 nodes and 2 edges in the database
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with a confirmation body; a subsequent GET /api/projects/proj-1 returns HTTP 404; all nodes and edges associated with proj-1 are removed from the database

test_cases.title: Deleted project does not appear in project list
preconditions: Projects proj-1 and proj-2 exist; proj-1 has been deleted
test_cases.given: Projects proj-1 and proj-2 existed; DELETE /api/projects/proj-1 was already called successfully
test_cases.then: Response is HTTP 200 with a JSON array containing exactly 1 item (proj-2); proj-1 does not appear in the list

test_cases.title: Clock Gate passes through input frequency unchanged
preconditions: An in-memory graph with PLL pll-1 (output_freq=300), ClockGate cg-1, and IPBlock ip-1 connected pll-1→cg-1→ip-1 exists
test_cases.given: Graph has PLL pll-1 with output_freq=300MHz, ClockGate cg-1, and IPBlock ip-1 connected in chain pll-1:out→cg-1:in, cg-1:out→ip-1:in
test_cases.then: cg-1 computed frequency is 300.000MHz (pass-through, same as input) and ip-1 operating frequency is 300.000MHz

test_cases.title: Clock Domain passes through input frequency unchanged to downstream nodes
preconditions: An in-memory graph with PLL pll-1 (output_freq=250), ClockDomain cd-1, and IPBlock ip-1 connected pll-1→cd-1→ip-1 exists
test_cases.given: Graph has PLL pll-1 with output_freq=250MHz, ClockDomain cd-1, and IPBlock ip-1 connected in chain pll-1:out→cd-1:in, cd-1:out_0→ip-1:in
test_cases.then: cd-1 computed frequency is 250.000MHz (pass-through, same as input) and ip-1 operating frequency is 250.000MHz
